channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: 'It's ridiculous!' | Ryu holes out for eagle to co-lead Amundi Evian Championship
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Haeran Ryu holes out for an eagle at the sixth hole to take a co-lead at the Amundi Evian Championship.

item.title: DOUBLE BLOW! India get two early wickets on day two
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: India deliver a double blow to England, removing Maia Bouchier and Heather Knight early on day two of the Women's Test.

item.title: Moses Simon delivers blunt Super Eagles verdict on Eric Chelle’s new deal
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Paris FC winger Moses Simon has welcomed the Nigeria Football Federation's decision to hand Super Eagles head coach Eric Chelle a new long-term contract, Soccernet.ng reports. First vice-captain Simon says the Super Eagles players are united behind the Malian coach and believe he can take the nation

item.title: La La La Lyrics by Fave
item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: Fave Lyrics La La La by Fave is a romantic, dreamy song centered on love, intimacy, and escapism. The lyrics express the excitement of falling deeply for someone, imagining a carefree future together away from everyday distractions. Through tender reflections and vivid romantic imagery, the song exp

item.title: Cross River seals three private health facilities
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: One of the sealed facilities was operated by a teacher without any medical training background, according to the government. The post Cross River seals three private health facilities appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Should Argentina take Messi off penalties?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Lionel Messi keeps rewriting World Cup history, but after his latest miss from the spot, should Argentina hand penalty duties to someone else?

item.title: Man City Sign Teenage Winger Monga On Five-Year Deal
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Monga made his English top-flight debut for the Foxes aged just 15 and has joined City despite interest from other leading clubs. The post Man City Sign Teenage Winger Monga On Five-Year Deal appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: NCDMB partners King-Fahji to train 50 Lagos youths on digitalised oil field operations, data analytics
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: By Sunday Ani The Nigerian Content Development and Monitoring Board (NCDMB), in collaboration with King-Fahji Nigeria Limited, has successfully concluded a training programme on digitalized oil field operations and data analytics for 50 youths in Badagry, Lagos State. The training, held in Badagry,

item.description: Arrangements have been concluded by the Plateau State Government to transform the state from a producer of raw potatoes into Nigeria's... The post Plateau moves to become Nigeria’s potato processing hub appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.description: Here are the countries with the highest number of goals scored at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post Countries with the highest number of goals at 2026 World Cup appeared first on NotjustOk.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Governor praised the collaborative efforts of the Nigerian Army, Nigerian Air Force, the Department of State Services, the Nigeria Police Force, and other security agencies that worked to secure the release of the victims.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Allied Peoples Movement (APM) governorship candidate in Lagos State, Prince Laja Adeoye, has praised Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, for what he described as courageous leadership and determination that led to the successful rescue of pupils and teachers abducted in Oriire Local Government Are

item.title: SSS releases journalist carrying drone after IPI Nigeria’s intervention
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The State Security Service (SSS), also called (DSS) released Ms Sodiq to IPI Nigeria's leadership at about 7:30 p.m. on Friday, July 10, 2026, following the organisation's intervention. item.title: Army denies Oyo government access to rescued school abduction victims
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Sources at the facility said some soldiers on duty turned back the delegation, citing instructions from higher authorities as the reason for restricting access. The post Army denies Oyo government access to rescued school abduction victims appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Courtois predicts team to win trophy ahead of France vs Spain semi-final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Belgium goalkeeper, Thibaut Courtois, has said the winner of the France vs Spain semi-final will win the 2026 World Cup. Courtois was speaking to reporters after they were eliminated in the quarter-final by Spain. Mikel Merino came off the bench to score the winner for La Roja, as they booked a last

item.title: Lady, 21, designs security app on phone to tackle emergencies, insecurity
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Twenty-one-year-old Halimat Onize Abdulazeez, a graduate of Civil Engineering from Nile University, Abuja, has designed an app for mobile phones to ensure rapid response for victims of insecurity and mitigate dangers during emergencies. The post Lady, 21, designs security app on phone to tackle emer

item.title: Actress MISS Peggy Ovire Shares New Photo After Re-Introducing Herself.......
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Actress Peggy shared this new photo just a day after announcing her new status, confirming that her divorce from fellow actor Freddie Leonard has been finalised.She looks innocently amazing in white......

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Jacob Ajom The 2026 FIFA World Cup has exposed new realities patterning to the growth, development and spread football enjoys among other sports. When FIFA announced the new expanded 48-team format – with Africa getting 9 automatic slots – for its flagship tournament, skeptics, includ

item.title: Oyo children, teachers freed on APC’s first re-election campaign day – Atiku criticises Tinubu

item.description: The former Vice President and Presidential Candidate of the African Democratic Congress, Atiku Abubakar, has noted that the release of Oyo kidnapped schoolchildren and teachers on Friday is a huge relief but expressed concerns over what he described as a political undertone. In a statement

item.title: Before Moniepoint and Opay, this fintech came first
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: In the latest episode of the Money Brief Podcast: Corporate Stories, host Ugo Obichukwu revisits the story of FlashMeCash, a GSM-based money transfer platform launched by First Atlantic Bank in 2002 and widely regarded as one of Nigeria’s earliest fintech innovations. item.title: Fubara presents N1.85 trillion budget for 2026 fiscal year
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The governor presented the budget to the Rivers State House of Assembly on Friday. The post Fubara presents N1.85 trillion budget for 2026 fiscal year appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Beauty Enyo-Ojo is a first-class graduate of the Federal University, Lokoja, in Kogi State. In this interview with IBRAHIM OBANSA, she recounts her remarkable journey from trekking to lectures and relying on borrowed phones to graduating with First-Class Honours in Economics as the Overall Best Grad

item.title: Sheikh Gumi kicks as federal lawmaker proposes killing repentant terrorists
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Islamic cleric, Sheikh Ahmad Gumi has condemned a proposal by a federal lawmaker, Yusuf Adamu Gagdi to execute so-called repentant terrorists and criminals instead of rehabilitating them The post Sheikh Gumi kicks as federal lawmaker proposes killing repentant terrorists appeared first on The Sun Ni

item.title: ASUU Begins Indefinite Strike Over Unpaid May, June Salaries At AAUA
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎The Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU), Adekunle Ajasin University, Akungba-Akoko (AAUA), Ondo State chapter, has commenced an indefinite withdrawal of services over the non-payment of lecturers’ May and June 2026 salaries. ‎The union said the industrial action took effect at 11:59 p

item.title: Playing golf with Trump surreal - Kane
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England captain Harry Kane says he once played golf with United States President Donald Trump, describing the experience as "surreal".

item.title: Torrential downpour pulls down house, kills five in Calabar

item.description: "Neighbours came out this morning to behold the horrible sight and were helping to remove the bodies from under the rubble. The sight was too much for me I had to leave the place". item.title: In Trinidad and Tobago, where Africa and India meet
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: But I also discovered that I have many Caribbean cousins and that one of my “ancestral journeys,” which is Ancestry’s term for places where people with whom one shares distant common ancestors live, is “Afro-Caribbean Peoples.”

item.description: Norway striker Erling Haaland’s remarkable performances at the FIFA World Cup have inspired hundreds of Peruvian parents to name their newborn children after the prolific forward. According to Peru’s National Registry of Identification and Civil Status (RENIEC), 468 children now carry the surname Ha

item.title: EU Orders Meta to Modify ‘Addictive’ Facebook and Instagram Features

item.description: The European Union has warned tech giant Meta that it must alter features on Facebook and Instagram described by regulators as “addictive,” or face massive financial penalties under the bloc’s Digital Services Act (DSA).   In its preliminary findings released on Friday, the European

item.title: Kane Hails ‘Machine’ Haaland, Insists England’s Focus Is World Cup Glory
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: England captain Harry Kane has described Norway striker Erling Haaland as “a machine” and “a beast” ahead of their eagerly anticipated FIFA World Cup quarter-final showdown, but insisted the pair are very different forwards. The two prolific goalscorers will go head-to-head on Saturday as England an

item.title: FG’s Death Threat To Terrorists Families Forced Oyo Victims Release — Operative

item.description: A former operative of the Department of State Services (DSS), Seyi Adetayo, has attributed the rescue of the 49 abducted pupils, teachers and caregivers in Oyo State to an intelligence-driven operation that targeted the families and close associates of members of the terrorist group, Ansarul, rather

item.title: Military Confirms Casualties In Rescue Of Kidnapped Oyo Schoolchildren, Teachers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Military has revealed that security forces suffered casualties in the operations that led to the rescue of kidnapped Oyo School children and teachers. Security forces on 10 July 2026 announced the rescue of 44 pupils and teachers kidnapped by terrorists from Oriire local government area

item.title: 44 Rescued Pupils, Teachers To Be Handed Over To Makinde, Families, Says 2 Div. Army
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigerian Army has disclosed that the rescued victims of the May 15, 2026 abduction in the Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State are currently undergoing medical attention at an undisclosed hospital in Ibadan and will soon be handed over to Governor Seyi Makinde and be reunited with their fam

item.title: Dele Alake and the dawn of Nigeria’s lithium revolution
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Dr. Felix Babatunde Ale For decades, Nigeria’s vast solid mineral resources remained one of the country’s most underutilised economic assets. Despite possessing commercially viable deposits of lithium, gold, tin, limestone, iron ore and several other strategic minerals, the The

item.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?

item.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

item.title: Land sold for Kushner-backed Albania resort suspected of forged deeds
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Albanian prosecutors probe forged deeds tied to Kushner resort land as protests over the project intensify.

item.title: Spain: Wildfires that killed 12 begin to ease (officials)
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: A wildfire that has killed at least 12 people in southern Spain showed signs of easing on Saturday, allowing firefighters to directly attack the flames for the first time, officials said. Our correspondent Sarah Morris tells us more.

item.title: Why was Ashleigh Plumptre left out of the Super Falcons WAFCON 2026 squad?
item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: Super Falcons defender Ashleigh Plumptre has been omitted from Nigeria's squad for the 2026 Women's Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON), with her ongoing recovery from injury understood to be the reason behind her absence. The 28-year-old has been sidelined since February after suffering a foot injury th

item.title: IK Ogbonna Debunks Alleged Romance Scam Report

item.description: Nollywood actor IK Ogbonna has taken to his social media page to debunk his involvement in a viral alleged romance scam. item.title: South Africa Begin Search For New Coach As Broos Confirms Exit
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The South African Football Association (SAFA) has begun the search for a new national team coach following confirmation that Hugo Broos will step down after the 2026 FIFA World Cup. A senior SAFA official told AFP on Friday that several applications had already been received, with former Bafana Bafa

item.title: Court Remands DJ Chicken In Kirikiri Over Threat To Kill Seyi Tinubu
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nigerian Disc Jockey and content creator, Ademola Abiodun, popularly known as DJ Chicken, has been arraigned before a magistrate’s court in Ogba, Lagos, over alleged threats to kill Seyi Tinubu, the son of President Bola Tinubu. Saturday Telegraph reports that the defendant appeared before M.F. Onam

item.title: NAF Strikes Terrorists Enclave In Borno
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Air Force (NAF), through the Air Component of Operation HADIN KAI (OPHK), has executed precision air strikes that devastated a terrorist enclave at Yaganari in the Gezuwa general area of Borno State. A statement by the NAF Spokesman Air Cdre Ehimen Ejodame said the Air Component on 10 J

item.title: France roar back with 30 straight points to beat Australia in Nations Championship
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Aaron Grandidier-Nkanang scored two tries and Romain Ntamack orchestrated a big comeback as France rallied from a nine-point half-time deficit to clinch a 42-26 win over Australia on Saturday in the second round of the rugby Nations Championship.

item.title: Kenya court to rule on Rastafarians' cannabis petition

item.description: On July 15, Kenya's High Court is set to deliver a landmark ruling. For years, Rastafarians — whose faith is officially recognised in Kenya — have been seeking the legalisation of cannabis, which they consider essential to their religious practice. The decision could have far-reaching consequences,

item.title: Mutfwang Flags Off Distribution Of 500 Truckloads Of Subsidised Fertiliser
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Governor Caleb Mutfwang of Plateau State on Friday officially flagged off the distribution of 500 truckloads of subsidised fertiliser for the 2026 farming season in Jos South Local Government Area. This is as he reaffirms his administration’s unwavering commitment to strengthening agricultural

item.title: Peter Obi reacts to rescue of abducted Oyo schoolchildren, teachers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has reacted to the rescue of the schoolchildren and teachers abducted in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State. In a post shared on his X account on Saturday, Obi expressed joy over the victims’ freedom and commended

item.title: EACOP: Ugandan farmers sue UK company in London
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Another legal setback for the massive EACOP oil pipeline. Four Ugandan farmers whose land lies directly along the pipeline's route, which is intended to connect Uganda to Tanzania, filed a lawsuit this week in London's High Court against the British company behind the project. Our correspondent Auro

item.title: Japaul Gold’s pre-tax profit falls 13% in 2025 despite 28% revenue growth
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Japaul Gold & Ventures Plc reported a group pre-tax profit of N1.30 billion for the year ended 31 December 2025, representing a 13.19% decline from the N1.50 billion recorded in 2024. The post Japaul Gold’s pre-tax profit falls 13% in 2025 despite 28% revenue growth appeared first on Nairametri

item.title: Abiodun presents Adeola to Ijebu royal fathers, says equity, competence guided his choice
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Governor Abiodun sought the traditional rulers’ support for the APC governorship candidate ahead of the 2027 general election The post Abiodun presents Adeola to Ijebu royal fathers, says equity, competence guided his choice appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Sudan: EU calls for terrorist listing of RSF
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The European Parliament adopted a milestone resolution on July 8 that addresses the armed conflict in Sudan and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) attacks in El Obeid, capital of North Kordofan. Our correspondent Bastien Renouil tells us more.

item.title: Caverton reports pre-tax loss of N13.87 billion in 2025 as revenue declines
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Caverton Offshore Support Group Plc has released its 2025 audited results for the period ended December 31, 2025, reporting a pre-tax loss of N13.87 billion, representing a 74.15% reduction from the N53.67 billion loss recorded in 2024. The post Caverton reports pre-tax loss of N13.87 billion in 202

item.title: Spain into World Cup semi-final with France after late strike against Belgium
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Spain struck late to beat Belgium 2-1 on Friday and move onto a blockbuster World Cup semi-final against France after a game decided by a goalkeeping error from Senne Lammens.

item.title: Trump reacts to reports of fresh Iranian plot to assassinate him
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: United States President Donald Trump has responded to reports of an alleged new Iranian plot to assassinate him... The post Trump reacts to reports of fresh Iranian plot to assassinate him appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: ‘One chair will remain empty forever’ – Slain Oyo teacher’s family reacts to rescue
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "He was taken from us, and his life was brutally cut short. Our joy is incomplete because one chair at our table will remain empty forever. The post ‘One chair will remain empty forever’ – Slain Oyo teacher’s family reacts to rescue appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Oriire Kidnap Victims’ Rescue: Abiodun hails Tinubu, Makinde, security agencies
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Governor Abiodun commended the gallantry, professionalism and exceptional courage of the security agencies whose coordinated efforts made the successful operation possible. He praised the Armed Forces, the Nigeria The post Oriire Kidnap Victims’ Rescue: Abiodun hails Tinubu, Makinde, security agenci

item.title: China evacuates over one million as Typhoon Bavi nears
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description:
More than 900,000 people have fled their homes in China, officials said, as an approaching typhoon lashed northern Taiwan and Japan's remote southwestern islands on Saturday, toppling trees and leaving tens of thousands without power.
Typhoon Bavi is expected to make landfall in China early on Sun
